checks.a_analysis: The JSON content was compared against each page of the source PDF. Every section title and text element in the output corresponds to content present in the source document. No fabricated text or headings were identified.

The URLs present in the JSON ('https://it.nc.gov/gicc-annual-report-FY-20-21' on page 2 and 'www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback' on page 13) match the source document exactly. No fabricated contact information was found.
All 13 pages of the source document are represented in the 'sections' array of the JSON. No pages or major sections were omitted during the conversion.

The table in Section 1 captures all the informational components from the right-hand column of the cover page (Page 1) of the source PDF, including the council name, report title, and the 'Presented to' list. No rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The textual content within each section of the JSON corresponds closely to the source material. Lists, headings, and labels under icons are preserved across all pages.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Textual information is captured accurately with high fidelity. There are no wording changes that alter meaning or incorrect numerical data.

checks.a_analysis: Each page of the source document corresponds to exactly one section in the output JSON. The text content within each section correctly reflects the content of its respective page without repetition. For example, the list of names and titles on the cover page appears only once in Section 1, and the URLs on pages 2 and 13 are not duplicated across other sections.

The output uses a flat hierarchy of sections, where each slide title from the source PDF is treated as a top-level section title. This is a consistent and logical mapping for a presentation deck where each slide represents a main topic. There are no skipped levels or incorrectly nested subsections (all 'subsections' arrays are empty).

The output contains one table in Section 1, which corresponds to the column of text on the right side of the cover page. The table is used to structure the agency name, report year, and 'Presented to' list. It correctly preserves the row structure for these three blocks of information as seen in the source.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the original document. On the cover page (Section 1), the left-hand column is processed before the right-hand column, which is a standard accessibility practice. On subsequent slides, the content is processed top-to-bottom, matching the visual hierarchy intended by the creators.

The lists on page 3 (Outline) are correctly converted into an unordered list with proper nesting for the 'Accomplishments' section and its sub-items. The highlights on pages 4 through 7, which are visually presented as distinct boxes, are appropriately converted into unordered list items, preserving their structural relationship.
The government URLs present on pages 2 and 13 are accurately captured as text within the paragraph elements. No fabricated links were added, and no existing URLs from the source were omitted.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully follows the linear organization of the source PDF. The multi-page PDF document is converted into a single-column web page layout which is appropriate for accessibility. Content from the cover page sidebar is correctly placed into a 'Document Presentation Details' section. The flow of information matches the PDF's intent page-by-page (Cover, Link, Outline, Highlights, Collaboration sections, Questions).

The source PDF does not contain traditional grid tables, but it does have a sidebar on the first page with key-value pairs (Presented to, Governor, etc.). The HTML output has correctly structured this information into a 'Document Presentation Details' table that is visually clear and readable.

Meaningful images and icons from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. The large aerial photo from the cover, the NCDIT logo, and the various section icons (e.g., for 'Seamless Parcels', 'Orthoimagery Program', 'Elevation Data') are represented by image placeholders (green boxes in the screenshot) or actual renders in their corresponding sections. The final 'Questions?' photo is also present.
There is no evidence of body content duplication. Each section and paragraph from the PDF appears exactly once in the rendered HTML output in its logical sequence.
